description: SQL injection vulnerability via unsanitized user input in query construction
remediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements instead of string concatenation

description: Reflected cross-site scripting through unescaped user input in HTML output
remediation: Encode all user-supplied data before rendering in HTML context

description: Path traversal allowing access to files outside intended directory
remediation: Validate and sanitize file paths, use allowlists for permitted directories

description: Hardcoded credentials or API keys embedded in source code
remediation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager for sensitive values

description: Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data allowing remote code execution
remediation: Avoid deserializing untrusted data; use safe serialization formats like JSON

description: Server-side request forgery allowing internal network access
remediation: Validate and restrict URLs to allowlisted domains; block internal IPs

description: Weak or missing authentication mechanisms allowing unauthorized access
remediation: Implement multi-factor authentication and proper session management

description: OS command injection via unsanitized input passed to system calls
remediation: Use parameterized APIs instead of shell commands; validate and sanitize all inputs

description: Weak TLS configuration or disabled certificate validation
remediation: Use TLS 1.2+ with strong cipher suites; always validate certificates

description: Container configuration allowing privilege escalation or host access
remediation: Run containers as non-root; drop capabilities; use read-only filesystems

description: Insecure direct object reference allowing unauthorized resource access
remediation: Implement proper authorization checks for all object references

description: Unvalidated redirect allowing phishing attacks via trusted domain
remediation: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of trusted domains
